customerUpdate: Hi Aisha, we've assigned Zava Field Assessors (General-purpose claims assessment) to help with your claim. They'll be in touch shortly to confirm one of these times: Fri 08 May, 10:00 AM, Mon 11 May, 2:00 PM, Mon 11 May, 9:00 AM. Work order WO-20260507-DEMO01 has been dispatched and we'll keep you posted on progress. Reply to this email if any of those times don't suit and we'll rebook.
agentNotes:
1. **Recommended Supplier**  
**RentRight Hire Cars** — **AUD 65/day**  
**Why:** Best-priced suitable match in the approved network, strong **4.6/5** rating, and fastest **1-day SLA**. This fits the claim priority because the customer relies on the vehicle daily for work and needs mobility support quickly.  
**Repair path note:** For physical repairs, **Citywide Auto Body (AUD 4,200, 5-day SLA)** is the lowest repair quote, while **Apex Smash Repairs (AUD 4,950, 3-day SLA, 4.8/5)** offers faster turnaround and preferred-network/OEM parts.

2. **Appointment Options**  
Recommended immediate rental-car fulfilment options:  
- **Option 1:** Thu 7 May, **12:00–2:00 pm**  
- **Option 2:** Thu 7 May, **4:00–6:00 pm**  
- **Option 3:** Fri 8 May, **8:00–10:00 am**  

Recommended next step for repairs/assessment:  
- Arrange **vehicle assessment within 1–3 business days** and confirm repairer selection after assessment.  
- If customer prefers speed/quality over lowest repair cost, steer to **Apex Smash Repairs**; if lowest repair price is priority, use **Citywide Auto Body**.

3. **Quote Request PDF**  
Download here:  
https://app-zc-backend.azurewebsites.net/supplier/quote-request/98c170adb3b3.pdf

4. **Supplier Status**  
- **Rental support:** Ready to dispatch to **RentRight Hire Cars** under urgent mobility need.  
- **SLA position:** On track if booked today; no overdue items identified.  
- **Assessment suppliers on file:**  
  - Zava Field Assessors — SLA **3 days**  
  - Independent Assessor Network — SLA **5 days**

5. **Quote Comparison**  
- **RentRight Hire Cars** — AUD **65/day** | Rating **4.6** | SLA **1 day** | Best mobility option  
- **Citywide Auto Body** — AUD **4,200** | Rating **4.4** | SLA **5 days** | Lowest repair price  
- **Apex Smash Repairs** — AUD **4,950** | Rating **4.8** | SLA **3 days** | Faster, preferred network, OEM parts

6. **Customer Update Draft**  
Hi Aisha — we’ve arranged priority supplier support for your claim and are now moving to book a rental vehicle so you can stay on the road for work. Current booking options are today 12:00–2:00 pm or 4:00–6:00 pm, or tomorrow 8:00–10:00 am. We’ll also progress the repair assessment and confirm the repairer with you shortly. Please reply with your preferred timeslot.  

7. **Human Approval Required**  
**No** — supplier selection is within the approved network and the recommended immediate dispatch is the **best-priced suitable option**.
agentInput:
APPROVED CLAIM — READY FOR SUPPLIER COORDINATION
================================================
Claim number: CLM-20260505-DEMO01
Customer: Aisha Khan (aisha.khan@example.com, 0433 220 901)
Policy: MOTOR-77881
Claim type: Motor — Third-party rear-end
Incident date: 2026-05-05
Incident location: Cnr Glenferrie Rd & Burwood Rd, Hawthorn VIC
Estimated loss: AUD 6,200
Urgency: Medium — Customer relies on the vehicle daily for work (community nurse). Prioritise a rental car booking with the Supplier Coordinator. No vulnerability flags.
Preferred contact: SMS

Incident description:
Stationary at a red light when rear-ended by a third-party Toyota Corolla; other driver admitted fault. Bumper and tailgate damage; vehicle drivable.

AVAILABLE SUPPLIERS (from Zava's approved network)
==================================================
- Zava Field Assessors — General-purpose claims assessment; Cnr Glenferrie Rd & Burwood Rd, Hawthorn VIC; rating 4.5/5; SLA 3 days
- Independent Assessor Network — Specialist on-call assessors; Cnr Glenferrie Rd & Burwood Rd, Hawthorn VIC; rating 4.4/5; SLA 5 days

MCP TOOL RESULTS (pre-resolved for you in this session)
=======================================================
The following MCP tool calls have already been executed by the Supplier Coordination service on your behalf — you do NOT need to call them again. Use these results directly when composing your response.

▶ lookupSuppliers(claimType, location) result:
{
  "claimType": "Motor \u2014 Third-party rear-end",
  "location": "Cnr Glenferrie Rd \u0026 Burwood Rd, Hawthorn VIC",
  "count": 3,
  "suppliers": [
    {
      "name": "RentRight Hire Cars",
      "specialty": "Like-for-like rental vehicles",
      "location": "National",
      "rating": 4.6,
      "slaDays": 1,
      "quoteAmount": 65,
      "quoteCurrency": "AUD",
      "notes": "Per-day hire rate."
    },
    {
      "name": "Citywide Auto Body",
      "specialty": "All-makes panel beating",
      "location": "Melbourne, VIC",
      "rating": 4.4,
      "slaDays": 5,
      "quoteAmount": 4200,
      "quoteCurrency": "AUD",
      "notes": "Lower price, longer SLA."
    },
    {
      "name": "Apex Smash Repairs",
      "specialty": "Modern vehicle panel \u0026 paint",
      "location": "Melbourne, VIC",
      "rating": 4.8,
      "slaDays": 3,
      "quoteAmount": 4950,
      "quoteCurrency": "AUD",
      "notes": "Preferred network; OEM parts."
    }
  ],
  "bestPrice": {
    "name": "RentRight Hire Cars",
    "quoteAmount": 65,
    "quoteCurrency": "AUD"
  }
}

▶ generateQuoteRequestPdf(...) result:
{
  "id": "98c170adb3b3",
  "fileName": "Zava-QuoteRequest-CLM20260505DEMO01-98c170adb3b3.pdf",
  "byteLength": 77731,
  "createdAt": "2026-05-07T02:34:32.6252111+00:00",
  "downloadUrl": "https://app-zc-backend.azurewebsites.net/supplier/quote-request/98c170adb3b3.pdf",
  "relativeUrl": "/supplier/quote-request/98c170adb3b3.pdf"
}

TASK
====
Using the pre-resolved MCP tool results above:
1. Recommend the best-priced supplier from lookupSuppliers.suppliers (the cheapest suitable match — see lookupSuppliers.bestPrice for the pre-computed pick) and briefly justify the choice on price, rating and SLA.
2. Surface the quote-request PDF download link (generateQuoteRequestPdf.downloadUrl) in your response so the operator can click to download it.
3. Propose appointment options, dispatch the work order, and draft a short plain-English update for the customer.

tools.name: settlement_requestPaymentApproval
tools.description: Raise a payment-approval request and post an Adaptive Card to Microsoft Teams so a human can approve or reject release. Returns the approvalId; this MUST be called before releasePayment.

claimNumber.description: Claim number the payment is for.

reason.description: Free-text reason / cross-check summary to show in the Teams card.

tools.name: compareQuotes
tools.description: Compare two or more parsed quotes (the output of analyzeQuote, or any equivalent JSON). Returns a markdown comparison table, a Mermaid bar-chart diagram of the totals, and a list of flagged anomalies (large total spread, missing line items, line items priced more than 25% above the median).

quotesJson.description: JSON array of quote objects. Each quote should have at least: vendor (string), total (number), lineItems (array of {description, amount}). The output of analyzeQuote is accepted directly — pass either the full result objects or just the .quote payloads.

tools.name: generateClaimExcel
tools.description: Generate an Excel (.xlsx) workbook for a loss-adjuster case. The workbook contains: 1) Claim Summary, 2) Quote Comparison (one row per line item per vendor), 3) Recommendations. Saves the file under wwwroot/loss-adjuster/output/ and returns its download URL.

claimNumber.description: The claim number, e.g. CL-1001. Used in the file name.

claimSummaryJson.description: JSON object with the claim summary fields to include on the Claim Summary sheet (any flat object — keys become labels, values become cell values).

quotesJson.description: Same JSON array of quotes accepted by compareQuotes — used to populate the Quote Comparison sheet.

recommendationsJson.description: Optional JSON object of recommendations: { recommendedReserve, recommendedVendor, notes, humanApprovalRequired }. Pass an empty object {} if none.

tools.name: settlement_calculateSettlement
tools.description: Run the settlement calculation transparently: payable = min(approved, policyLimit) - excess - depreciation - priorPayments (floored at 0).

policyLimit.description: Policy limit applicable to this claim.

priorPayments.description: Prior payments already released against this claim.

tools.name: analyzeQuote
tools.description: Fetch a contractor repair-quote document (JSON, markdown, plain text, or absolute path under /loss-adjuster/samples/) and return its parsed line items, totals and metadata as JSON. Use this to read each quote on a complex claim before comparing them.

documentUrl.description: URL or absolute path to the quote document. Absolute paths starting with '/' are resolved against the app's wwwroot (e.g. /loss-adjuster/samples/quote-acme-restoration.json). Public http(s) URLs are also supported.

tools.name: generateQuoteRequestPdf
tools.description: Generate a Zava Insurance quote-request PDF for a claim and supplier. Returns a JSON object with a downloadUrl that the operator can click to fetch the PDF. Use this once a supplier has been selected from lookupSuppliers.

quoteAmount.description: Indicative quote amount in major currency units (e.g. 4950).

tools.name: settlement_getApprovalStatus
tools.description: Look up the current status of a payment-approval request created by requestPaymentApproval.

tools.name: settlement_checkAuthorityLimit
tools.description: Check a payable amount against the Settlement Officer authority limit. Amounts at or above the threshold always require human approval.

threshold.description: Authority threshold in AUD. Defaults to 5000.

tools.name: settlement_releasePayment
tools.description: Release the payment for a previously-approved request. Fails unless the approval is in the Approved state. Never call this without prior human approval.

tools.name: lookupSuppliers
tools.description: Look up Zava's approved-network supplier directory for a claim. Returns suppliers (with indicative quote, rating and SLA) ordered by ascending quote price so the cheapest match is first. Use this to pick the best-priced supplier before generating a quote request.

location.description: Optional incident location, used to bias the ordering (e.g. 'Sydney, NSW').

tools.name: settlement_matchInvoice
tools.description: Match a supplier invoice total against the approved scope on the claim. Returns whether the invoice is within tolerance and any variance.

claimNumber.description: Claim number the invoice belongs to.

approvedTotal.description: Approved scope total (in AUD, decimal). If omitted, the claim's estimated loss is used.

tolerance.description: Allowed variance percentage as a fraction (e.g. 0.05 for 5%).

tools.name: settlement_validatePayee
tools.description: Validate that the payee name on the payment matches the customer recorded on the claim. Use this before requesting payment approval.

claimNumber.description: Claim number minted at intake (e.g. ZC-2025-000123).

payeeName.description: Name of the payee that the payment will be released to.
